metadata.title: NV Center ODMR Field Map with Claude

beats.narration_text: Hola. This is Liam, in for Bear. One Claude prompt. A live interactive simulation of odmr two lorentzian dips.
Quantum mechanics is full of non-obvious results. Today's: ODMR two Lorentzian dips. Key checkable number: B=10→56 MHz splitting. Let us build the demonstration.

props.prompt: Build me an interactive D3 simulation of odmr two lorentzian dips. Use D3 version 7 from CDN. Single-file HTML, all physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Add at least one slider or control. Teardown palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. D3 version 7. All the physics runs in the browser. No server. No build step.
Here is the baseline — the simulation at rest. ODMR two Lorentzian dips.
Now interact with the control — drag the slider, click the toggle. Watch the physics respond.
The simulation updates in real time. B=10→56 MHz splitting. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live quantum mechanics demonstration. ODMR two Lorentzian dips. Bear reviews these in the morning and ships the ones that pass.
Your turn. Extend this vol4 nv odmr field map simulation. Add a second control that changes [parameter]. Show how it affects the result.

props.prompt: Extend this vol4 nv odmr field map simulation. Add a second control that changes [parameter]. Show how it affects the result.
